vol_description: Infants aged 1-6 months of age were video-taped in their home (natural) environment during day-time hours. The goal was to capture tummy time (prone positioning). Video was collected using a Mobius dashcam and parents were instructed to turn the camera on in the morning and leave it running until the memory card filled up, usually 10-12 hours. Many other social and environmental interactions were captured. Our goal was to assess tummy time behavior including facilitators and barriers that served to extend or prematurely end a bout of tummy time. Some videos are not sound enabled, but most are.
